concerns.text: The plan assumes N=500 per group (1000 total) can be generated within 6 hours on a GitHub Actions Free Tier runner (2 CPU, ~7GB RAM) using LCM-LoRA. Even with LCM (4-8 steps), generating 1000 images on a 2-core CPU instance typically exceeds 6 hours (estimated 1-2 mins/image implies 16-33 hours). The plan relies on a 'pilot run' to reduce N, but the primary experimental design (N=500) is computationally infeasible under the stated constraints without a paid compute tier or a significantly smaller model (e.g., 64x64 resolution which breaks YOLOv8n evaluation). The dataset size (N=500) is inappropriate for the available free compute resources.

The plan targets 'GitHub Actions Free Tier' for the full N=1000 generation pipeline. This resource constraint is incompatible with the proposed diffusion model inference workload (even distilled/LCM variants) for the stated sample size. The dataset scale (1000 images) must be reduced to a feasible number (e.g., N=50-100) for the free tier, or the compute resource assumption must be changed to a paid tier, which violates the 'free-model + free-compute' constraint of the llmXive framework.
